---
title: "foundations"
subtitle: "Precision kinds and package-wide constants"
---

## Overview

The `precision` and `constants` modules are the shared base that every other
`fesm-utils` module builds on. `precision` defines the real kinds used
throughout the library — the working precision `wp`, plus explicit single
(`sp`) and double (`dp`) kinds. `constants` defines the package-wide domain
values: the missing value, error sentinels, mathematical constants, and
tolerances.

The two concerns are kept separate on purpose. Floating-point kinds live in
`precision`; domain values live in `constants`. As the `constants` header
notes, precision kinds must be sourced directly from `precision` — they are
**not** re-exported from `constants`.

## precision

`precision` defines three integer kind parameters:

| Name | Definition | Meaning |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `dp` | `kind(1.d0)` | Double-precision real kind |
| `sp` | `kind(1.0)` | Single-precision real kind |
| `wp` | `sp` | Working precision (the default kind for library reals) |

The working precision `wp` is selected by aliasing one of the explicit kinds.
It is currently set to single precision (`wp = sp`); changing that one line
retargets the working precision of the whole library.

```fortran
use precision, only: wp

real(wp) :: x
```

Use `sp` or `dp` directly when a routine must pin a variable to a specific
precision regardless of the working precision.

## constants

`constants` uses `wp` and `dp` from `precision` for its own declarations but
keeps them `private`, so they are not re-exported — source kinds from
`precision`. Values default to working precision (`wp`) except where a
double-precision form is explicitly required (geometry, weights, and other
double-only internals).

| Name | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `mv` | Missing value, working precision (`-9999.0_wp`) |
| `mv_dp` | Missing value, double precision (`-9999.0_dp`) |
| `ERR_DIST` | Error distance sentinel — a very large distance (`1E8_dp`) |
| `ERR_IND` | Error index sentinel (`-1`) |
| `pi` | Pi, computed as `2*acos(0)` in double precision |
| `degrees_to_radians` | Conversion factor `pi / 180` |
| `radians_to_degrees` | Conversion factor `180 / pi` |
| `TOL` | General tolerance (`1e-8_wp`) |
| `TOL_UNDERFLOW` | Underflow tolerance (`1e-15`, cast to `wp`) |

The missing value `mv` (`-9999.0`) is the library-wide flag for absent or
invalid data, with `mv_dp` supplying the same sentinel at double precision for
the double-only internals. The error sentinels `ERR_DIST` and `ERR_IND` mark
failed distance and index lookups: `ERR_DIST` is a deliberately huge distance
(`1E8`) so that a bad match never wins a nearest-neighbour comparison, and
`ERR_IND = -1` is an out-of-range index.

The mathematical constants are all double precision. `pi` is computed as
`2*acos(0)` rather than hard-coded, and `degrees_to_radians` /
`radians_to_degrees` are derived from it for angle conversions. The tolerances
`TOL` (`1e-8`) and `TOL_UNDERFLOW` (`1e-15`) are working precision; `TOL` is the
general comparison tolerance and `TOL_UNDERFLOW` guards against underflow.

## Usage note

A module that needs both the working kind and the domain constants pulls each
from its own module:

```fortran
use precision, only: wp
use constants, only: mv, pi, TOL

real(wp) :: value

if (abs(value - mv) < TOL) then
    ! value is the missing-value flag
end if
```

## See also

- [coords](coords.qmd)
- [tsgen](tsgen.qmd)
